tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, elevated creatinine, and high chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment and electrolyte imbalance. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible diabetic nephropathy and/or urinary tract pathology. HbA1c is elevated, consistent with suboptimal glycemic control. Recommend further evaluation for renal function, diabetes management, and assessment for urinary tract infection or other urinary pathology.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
